description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a high-wing configuration and a fixed tricycle landing gear. The fuselage is primarily white with orange accents along the edges and tail, and the cockpit features dark-tinted windows. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer. The wings are straight and lack winglets, with a simple, utilitarian design suitable for general aviation. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a nose wheel, all fixed and exposed. The overall appearance suggests a light aircraft commonly used for training, recreation, or short-distance travel.
description: A small, high-wing, single-engine airplane with orange and white coloring, fixed tricycle landing gear, and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, nose wheel, and the full span of the high-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Left-side perspective highlighting the cockpit windows, wing structure, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the main landing gear.
description_view_3: Right-side perspective showing the propeller, cockpit, and the high-wing configuration with visible landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no winglets or visible dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
